channel.title: Small-Ai-Models on TinyWeights.dev
channel.description: Recent content in Small-Ai-Models on TinyWeights.dev

item.title: GGUF vs ONNX vs MLX: Which Model Format Should You Use for Local Inference?
item.description: GGUF, ONNX, and MLX compared: what each format is for, which quantization levels to pick, and how to choose the right one for your hardware.

item.title: Ollama vs LM Studio vs llama.cpp: Which Local AI Runtime Should You Use?
item.description: Ollama vs LM Studio vs llama.cpp compared: setup, performance, API support, and which runtime to pick for your hardware and use case in 2026.

item.title: The Best Small Language Models in 2026: A Practical Comparison
item.description: A practical guide to the best small language models in 2026—Phi-4-mini, SmolLM3-3B, Gemma 3 4B, Qwen3, and more—with benchmarks, VRAM requirements, and honest use-case picks.

item.title: Qwen3.5-0.8B: A Multimodal Thinking Model That Fits in 1 Gigabyte
item.description: Qwen3.5-0.8B packs multimodal vision, a 262K context window, and an on-demand thinking mode into a 1GB file. Here's the architecture behind it and how to run it locally.

item.title: Qwen3-Coder-Next: Run a Frontier-Level Coding Agent Locally on Consumer Hardware
item.description: Alibaba's Qwen3-Coder-Next packs 80B total parameters into a 3B-active MoE, scores 70.6% on SWE-Bench Verified, and runs on a single RTX 5090 or 128GB Mac Studio.

item.title: Gemma 4: Taking Agentic Workflows to the Edge
item.description: DeepMind's Gemma 4 brings 128K contexts, native 'thinking', and powerful multimodal agentic workflows to constrained devices and local hardware.

item.title: Deep Dive: Running Reka Edge Locally for Frontier-Level Vision AI on Mac and PC
item.description: An in-depth look at Reka Edge, the 7B multimodal vision-language model. Learn about its ConvNeXt V2 architecture, token efficiency, and how to run it locally on consumer hardware.

item.title: AI in Your Pocket: How Liquid AI’s Apollo App Lets You Run Chatbots Completely Offline
item.description: Discover how Liquid AI's Apollo app and LEAP platform bring fully private, offline small language models (SLMs) straight to your smartphone.

item.title: The 'Small' Model That Does It All: How Mistral Small 4's Unified Architecture Kills the Need for Specialized AI
item.description: Discover how Mistral Small 4 unifies reasoning, multimodal, and coding into an efficient 119B MoE model that runs locally and slashes API costs.

item.title: Out of the Cloud, Into the Wild: How Small AI Models and Physical AI Are Taking Over the Edge
item.description: Why the AI industry is pivoting from massive data centers to Small Language Models (SLMs) running locally on devices, factories, and 5G networks.

item.title: Fine-Tune Gemma 3 270M on Apple Silicon with MLX-LM and Python
item.description: A practical, step-by-step guide to fine-tuning Google's Gemma 3 270M on your Mac using Apple's MLX framework, LoRA, and Python.

item.title: GPT-5.4 Nano: The Fastest, Cheapest OpenAI Model Yet — What Developers Need to Know
item.description: GPT-5.4 nano is OpenAI's most efficient model yet — $0.20/M input tokens, 400k context, API-only. Here's what it can do and when to reach for it.

item.title: SmolLM3-3B: The Fully Open Small Language Model That Punches Way Above Its Weight
item.description: SmolLM3-3B from HuggingFace is a fully open 3B SLM with 128k context, dual-mode reasoning, and multilingual support. Here's how to run it locally.
